content:
This is causing crashes for me when compiled for release on Android on the current stable channel 1.22.6.
The second a user taps the input it crashes. 

Any work arounds? It's a very straightforward widget. Just a TextField and a button to return the text as a route argument..

EDIT:
Setting autofocus to true fixes it for now...
EDIT 2:
Scratch that just causes app to crash instantly upon navigating to route. No debug errors though.

Is there another widget to capture text input that I can use? Or can I not use flutter until this is fixed?

I have the _exact same_ issue as @FlashChaser

> This is causing crashes for me when compiled for release on Android on the current stable channel 1.22.6.
> The second a user taps the input it crashes.
> 
> Any work arounds? It's a very straightforward widget. Just a TextField and a button to return the text as a route argument..
> 
> EDIT:
> Setting autofocus to true fixes it for now...
> EDIT 2:
> Scratch that just causes app to crash instantly upon navigating to route. No debug errors though.
> 
> Is there another widget to capture text input that I can use? Or can I not use flutter until this is fixed?

May be the TextField element/widget is marked for rebuild or set state, then removed and a new one comes in but the focus is still on the one marked for rebuild?

For context started an app on the dev channel with firebase core 0.5.0 last year. A couple days ago I switched over everything to the 'null safety' firebase libraries and switched to the newest stable flutter version 2.0.3. I think I had the bachEdit errors back then, but now it seems to rebuild my entire material route tree. I tried Navigator.of(context).[pushName & push] and Navigator.push() and tried both with and without the schedulerbinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback method. I also tried to have them push either MaterialPageRoute or ModalRoute. I removed all keys, changed my app to try to not pass data with the offending classes (just a more isolated firebase bloc pattern for the classes). I tried changing 'controller:' and using a controller within onChange, i even removed the controller altogether. But it still seems to rebuild everything from the 'home:' of Material App when ever the TextField is selected.

In debugging (with Android studio) I set a break points all over (from main.dart to the TextField) and it seems that maybe when the TextField or TextFormField is clicked or focused on, it rebuilds or changes state but the focus continuously stays on it during the rebuild. I dont know enough about dirty elements/trees and rebuilds to really make a claim but:
may be the TextField element/widget is removed and a new one comes in but the focus is still on the one marked for rebuild? The error then comes in between the rebuild or new state of the textfeild? Maybe then the focus during the rebuild also causes a mark for rebuild during the rebuild/set state creating a loop that causes more issues?

Maybe a related issue, i started getting a new error 'setState() or markNeedsBuild() called during build.' in another area which i did not have before, I'll post some where else. But the issues may be related in a far back change in rebuild logic from a stable/dev channel in the past. But what do i know

Edit: dev 1.26.0-12.0.pre ( a706cd2  1/21/2021) was the last sdk that didnt cause rebuilding problems from the TextField
Oddly enough I just installed the flutter demo from the google play store. It crashes for me as well when clicking on text input... not ideal.
Is this never going to be solved? what's the point of having a whole develop framework that doesn't work with form fields? so many hours spent to develop a useless framework.

The problem continues. Computer Keyboard.

So okay I have found a solution for this in my use case.
My keyboard used to disappear as soon as it appeared on my screen. This not the bug in the framework, let us understand what happens in the background.
When the keyboard pops up, the screen needs to rebuild its components accordingly, so it forces a rebuild. Therefore the entire widgets are re-rendered including the TextFormField or TextField, which closes the Keyboard.
To solve this problem, we have to find a way to uniquely identify our widget which is rebuilding, and stop flutter from recreating that widget.
This can be done by using keys, first find the parent widget under which our TextFormField/TextField falls, and provide a unique key to it. Remember NOT to initialize the key on the key parameter of the widget. Instead declare it outside the build method and then use it. This makes sure that the unique does not change every time the build method is called.
I hope that this helps.
Thank You!

> So okay I have found a solution for this in my use case.
> My keyboard used to disappear as soon as it appeared on my screen. This not the bug in the framework, let us understand what happens in the background.
> When the keyboard pops up, the screen needs to rebuild its components accordingly, so it forces a rebuild. Therefore the entire widgets are re-rendered including the TextFormField or TextField, which closes the Keyboard.
> To solve this problem, we have to find a way to uniquely identify our widget which is rebuilding, and stop flutter from recreating that widget.
> This can be done by using keys, first find the parent widget under which our TextFormField/TextField falls, and provide a unique key to it. Remember NOT to initialize the key on the key parameter of the widget. Instead declare it outside the build method and then use it. This makes sure that the unique does not change every time the build method is called.
> I hope that this helps.
> Thank You!

I actually did this. But I also had to replace TextField with TextFormField and set a key for that widget outside build and also its parent widget(like set the key of that stateless widget from its parent as an argument). 
It worked, thanks!

> > So okay I have found a solution for this in my use case.
> > My keyboard used to disappear as soon as it appeared on my screen. This not the bug in the framework, let us understand what happens in the background.
> > When the keyboard pops up, the screen needs to rebuild its components accordingly, so it forces a rebuild. Therefore the entire widgets are re-rendered including the TextFormField or TextField, which closes the Keyboard.
> > To solve this problem, we have to find a way to uniquely identify our widget which is rebuilding, and stop flutter from recreating that widget.
> > This can be done by using keys, first find the parent widget under which our TextFormField/TextField falls, and provide a unique key to it. Remember NOT to initialize the key on the key parameter of the widget. Instead declare it outside the build method and then use it. This makes sure that the unique does not change every time the build method is called.
> > I hope that this helps.
> > Thank You!
> 
> I actually did this. But I also had to replace TextField with TextFormField and set a key for that widget outside build and also its parent widget(like set the key of that stateless widget from its parent as an argument).
> It worked, thanks!

Most welcome! Yes, it's all about understanding how flutter works and then making the changes to your use cases accordingly!

For anyone having this same problem, this solved it for me:

`WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback((_) => _textFieldController.clear());`

From:
[StackOverflow: Unhandled Exception: PlatformException(error, invalid selection start: 24, null, java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException: invalid selection start: 24](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/66985005/unhandled-exception-platformexceptionerror-invalid-selection-start-24-null)

This has always plagued me, and unfortunately, I've come to get used to it since I like having a more responsive emulator;  but if you change your emulator graphics from Hardware to Software it prevents the log spam (for me at least).
This is still a thing as of 08/08/21.

I am having this issue even after releasing the app via Google Play Store. Get same warnings in the log panel, but the bigger issue is that keyboard disappears and screen gets re-rendered as soon as user clicks on the TextFormField to edit it - tried all the solutions mentioned above and nothing works

EDIT: my issue with screen reloading was caused by having the TextField inside FutureBuilder. In case anyone is experiencing the same issue, the fix is to call the future from inside the initState method and only reference it in the FutureBuilder as described here: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/51224420/flutter-switching-to-tab-reloads-widgets-and-runs-futurebuilder

Still seems to be an issue; March 5 2022 - Flutter 2.10.2
On testing with code from the Material Components Basics - 101 demo, I observe the following behavior on Android emulator:

## Switching Focus
When tapping on a TextField to focus and start typing and
* Both Username and Password fields are empty, when switching from/to either field:
`I/AssistStructure( 5007): Flattened final assist data: 404 bytes, containing 1 windows, 3 views`

* Username field has content and Password field is empty:
    - Shifting focus from username field to password field by tapping:
    `W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): getSelectedText on inactive InputConnection`
`W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): getTextAfterCursor on inactive InputConnection`
    - Shifting focus from password field to username field by tapping:
    `I/AssistStructure( 5007): Flattened final assist data: 404 bytes, containing 1 windows, 3 views`

* Username field is empty and password field has content:
    - Shifting focus from username field to password field by tapping:
    `I/AssistStructure( 5007): Flattened final assist data: 404 bytes, containing 1 windows, 3 views`
    - Shifting focus from password field to username field by tapping:
    `
E/SpannableStringBuilder( 5007): SPAN_EXCLUSIVE_EXCLUSIVE spans cannot have a zero length`
sometimes followed by:
`I/AssistStructure( 5007): Flattened final assist data: 400 bytes, containing 1 windows, 3 views`

* Both Username and password fields have some text:
    - Shifting focus from username field to password field by tapping:
    `W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): getSelectedText on inactive InputConnection`
`W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): getTextAfterCursor on inactive InputConnection`
    - Shifting focus from password field to username field by tapping:
    `I/AssistStructure( 5007): Flattened final assist data: 404 bytes, containing 1 windows, 3 views`

## Typing
Using the emulator's keyboard seems to not give warnings when typing in either the Username or Password fields, atleast in my case.

However on each character typed (or deleted) using my PCs physical keyboard, in the Username field, the following warnings show up:

`I/TextInputPlugin( 5007): Composing region changed by the framework. Restarting the input method.`
`W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): getTextAfterCursor on inactive InputConnection`
`W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): getTextBeforeCursor on inactive InputConnection`
`W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): getSelectedText on inactive InputConnection`
`W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): getTextAfterCursor on inactive InputConnection`
`W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): beginBatchEdit on inactive InputConnection`
`W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): getTextBeforeCursor on inactive InputConnection`
`W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): endBatchEdit on inactive InputConnection`
`

Using the Physical keyboard to type in the password field does not generate any warnings

## Submitting
Pressing submit after typing in the Username field generates the following errors for both emulator keyboard and physical keyboard
EDIT: 'Pressing submit' meaning the submit button on the emulator keyboard or the 'Enter' key on PC's physical keyboard:

`I/TextInputPlugin( 5007): Composing region changed by the framework. Restarting the input method.`
`W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): getTextBeforeCursor on inactive InputConnection`
`W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): getSelectedText on inactive InputConnection`
`W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 5007): getTextAfterCursor on inactive InputConnection`
`
No warnings are thrown on doing the same with the Password field.


## Appendix
The code defining the Username and Password fields:
`
const TextField(
            decoration: InputDecoration(
              filled: true,
              labelText: 'Username',
            ),
          ),
`
`const TextField(
            decoration: InputDecoration(
              filled: true,
              labelText: 'Password',
            ),
            obscureText: true,
          ),
`

I couldn't arrive at a solution or identify the cause.
I'm not sure if any of this is consequential, but it's more data and could be helpful.
More power to the Flutter Team! Hope this gets fixed soon!

This issue comes when TextFiled is used in another Widget (outside the Scaffold Widget).

As a solution,

We can take "TextEditingController" as a parameter. And "TextEditingController" should be defined inside the Scaffold Widget.

![Screenshot 2023-01-25 044328](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/62515133/214442135-89daa1df-7abd-49c4-a181-0e12fc2ab438.jpg)
`keyboardType: TextInputType.visiblePassword` solves problems with changing the keyboard type (generally speaking, the logs will also become cleaner, but I didn’t care about them)

I checked this on two Android devices (Realme 6, Android 11, Galaxy Tab A7 Lite (Android 14)) but couldn't see the warning/error as reported. I'm on the latest Flutter stable 3.19.5.

If anyone still experiences this issue on the latest Flutter release, please file a new issue with device info (OS, model) and all other information indicated in the issue template (See bug issue template [here](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/new?assignees=&labels=&projects=&template=2_bug.yml)). ## Instance state not saved when app is killed by OS

## What is instance state, and why it exists
On Android, an [Activity](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/app/Activity.html) can be killed at any time by the system. This happens usually when Android needs memory when your Activity is not in the foreground, or because of a non-handled configuration change, such as a locale change.
To avoid the user having to restart what he did from scratch when Android killed the Activity, the system calls `onSaveInstanceState(…)` when the Activity is paused, where the app is supposed to save it's data in a [`Bundle`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/os/Bundle.html), and passes the saved bundle in both `onCreate(…)` and `onRestoreInstanceState(…)` when the task is resumed if the activity has been killed by the system.

## The issue about it in flutter
In the flutter apps I tried (Flutter Gallery, and the base project with the FAB tap counter), if I open enough apps to make Android kill the flutter app's Activity, all the state is lost when I come back to the flutter activity (while not having remove the task from recents).

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Install [Flutter Gallery](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=io.flutter.gallery&hl=en)
2. Open the device's settings, and in developer options, switch "Don't keep activities" on. (see the screenshot)
![dev_option](https://pbs.twimg.com/media/Cw6qdhfWQAAKo_J.jpg:small)
This will allow to simulate when Android kills Activities because it lacks memory and you're not in the foreground.
3. Open the Flutter Gallery app and go anywhere other than the main screen.
4. Go to the launcher by pressing the device's home button.
5. Press the overview button and return to Flutter Gallery. Here's the bug.

**What's expected:** The app is in the same state that where we left off, with untouched UI.
**What happens:** The activity is restarted from scratch, losing all the UI state, even really really long forms.
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/3427 is also likely related.
@eseidelGoogle That's right. In which format could the flutter Activity state be saved, if it can be at all as of the current version?

Right now we don't do anything to save anything.

The framework itself has very little state worth saving -- it's all animation and stuff like that -- so it may be that we always leave this up to the app to do. We should probably expose it at the Dart level though. (Right now it's only exposed at the Java level.)
